evidence_note: The company extensively applies modern equipment, including automatic fabric cutting machines, automated programming systems, and management software, to improve processes and increase production speed.

The company invested in advanced equipment such as large-scale programmable machines, automatic fiber filling systems, and automatic fabric cutting machines, applying modern technologies to increase production speed and reduce manual operations.

The company implemented a range of management improvements, including ISO 9001:2015 and ISO 14001:2015 systems, 5S practices, increased automation, and redesigned HR processes with KPI-based performance evaluation.

The company improved production by implementing ISO 9001:2015 and ISO 14001:2015 systems, promoting LEAN software and 5S practices, investing in modern automatic fabric cutting machines, and using solar energy to reduce waste.
AAT expanded into new sectors including industrial cluster investment, trading, services, and tourism, while shifting from simple outsourcing to a full-package manufacturing model (FOB).
The company expanded into new business areas, including warehouse leasing, frozen food distribution, and real estate.
The company announced its participation in developing a new real estate project as a 5-star hotel and resort apartment complex (Fivestar Odyssey) in Vũng Tàu.

Launched multiple high-quality new products, including durable industrial coatings to meet market demand, while investing in advanced production technologies and building a new factory in Long An, which is now fully operational.

The company expanded production and business of new products for civil and construction applications in 2022, while completing and putting into operation a new factory in Hải Sơn Industrial Park (Long An), equipped with advanced production technologies.

The company plans to develop and commercialize new products while investing in advanced technologies to meet future demand, alongside further investment in machinery and equipment to enhance and expand production at the new Hải Sơn (Long An) factory.

The company introduced new coating products such as zinc paint, economical PU paint, and anti-fingerprint coatings, while upgrading processes through investment in new grinding machines and packaging lines.

The company plans to launch new coating products, including zinc paint, economical PU paint, anti-fingerprint coatings, and low heavy-metal coatings, while continuing process improvements through investment in new grinding machines and packaging lines.

During the year, the company focused on increasing fiber production capacity and advancing ongoing real estate projects, while installing rooftop solar power systems at Gia Lễ and Nguyễn Đức Cảnh industrial zones and investing in infrastructure for An Ninh Industrial Park to support renewable energy development.
In 2022, through its affiliated company, the company expanded its green energy business by introducing a new product—solar panels.
The 2024 annual report highlights improvements in production through the adoption of the 5S model in management processes, reducing waste and enhancing productivity for over 300 factory workers.

The company expanded into residential real estate and services, developing townhouses, villas, and worker housing (Block CC6) in the industrial service center, while investing in a booster pumping station and upgrading environmental prevention and response infrastructure.

xpansion into residential and service development beyond industrial land leasing, while the company continued operating and upgrading water supply and wastewater treatment systems, delivering over 1.5 million m³ of water to enterprises in the industrial park.

The company continued completing service center subprojects to support the living needs of experts and workers in the industrial park, while maintaining and optimizing infrastructure systems and supplying over 1.2 million m³ of stable clean water to partners.

The company began operating and generating revenue from new offerings, including worker housing, a kindergarten, and commercial service units, while planning upgrades to the industrial park’s wastewater treatment plant (phase 2, 4,000 m³/day), along with independent collection and discharge systems.

The company plans to acquire 5–10 hectares of land for new real estate projects and continue leasing service center facilities (kindergarten, healthcare, cultural and educational services), while maintaining stable operations of wastewater treatment, collection systems, and booster stations to optimize utilities for tenants.

The company set strategic directions to invest in R&D for new products to meet market demand and boost revenue and profits, while strengthening trade promotion and upgrading production lines and technologies. It also invested in modern, synchronized equipment to automate processes, reduce manual work, and improve output and product quality.

The company maintained its focus on developing new products and enhancing quality, while promoting trade activities. Notably, in 2021 it invested nearly VND 21 billion in a 30-ton/hour boiler to reduce production disruption risks. It also continued investing in modern, synchronized technologies and equipment to advance automation and increase output.

The company’s development plan includes investing in R&D to diversify new products for market demand, while the Board approved new production lines and equipment upgrades to enhance efficiency and innovation. It also pursues continuous investment in modern technologies and equipment to drive automation and improve productivity.

The company aims to innovate packaging and product design, further develop existing products, and diversify new offerings to meet market demand, while investing in synchronized equipment to enhance output and quality. It also continues upgrading technologies and modern equipment to automate processes and reduce manual work.

The company’s development plan focuses on improving packaging and design, enhancing existing product lines, and diversifying new products to meet market demand, while maintaining stable factory operations, upgrading technology, improving quality, and optimizing costs; notably, the Bich Chi 2 Food Factory is in final acceptance and equipment installation for operation readiness.

The company accelerated the development and commercialization of deep-processed products, while undertaking a comprehensive restructuring by shifting from a two-tier management model (company–workshop) to a three-tier structure (company–factory/enterprise–workshop).
The company actively implemented technical improvements to reduce energy and fuel consumption, while beginning to use renewable energy to partially replace fossil fuels.
The company continued improving slag processing lines and implemented measures to reduce electricity consumption per unit of output, thereby lowering production costs.
The company conducted a pre-feasibility study for a ferromanganese project as a basis for diversifying its product portfolio.

The company researched and applied new reinforcement fabrics for conveyor belts to improve appearance and meet customer requirements, while adjusting production technology for oil-resistant belts to reduce blister defects and enhance adhesion.

The company successfully improved vulcanized belt splicing for conveyor belts, enhancing appearance and extending lifespan up to three times, while implementing a “3-on-site” production model and equipping materials to ensure pandemic safety.

The company optimized the adhesive formulation for steel-core conveyor belt splicing, increasing added value by 10–15% and improving joint quality, while converting DC motors to AC motors to reduce repair costs and prevent production disruptions.

The company researched and applied a new rubber compound formula and established production technology for ribbed conveyor belts, while deploying data display systems across workshops to support gradual digital transformation in manufacturing.

The company developed a new compound formula for L-shaped rubber profiles with 50 Shore A hardness and 76 rebound, while investing in time and process monitoring systems integrated with barcodes to track production.
The company approved investment in R&D for a new five-blade ceiling fan model, while applying and maintaining the ISO 9001:2015 quality management system and 5S practices supported by JICA.

The company continued developing and refining its five-blade ceiling fan line with competitive pricing, while improving quality, increasing labor productivity, and reducing costs to enhance competitiveness during the COVID-19 pandemic.

The Board of Directors directed investment in developing four new products for 2023—400 retractable fan, 400 wall fan, 230 wall fan, and 450 industrial floor fan—while promoting OEM manufacturing and diversifying export channels.

The company diversified its assembly product portfolio with 40 Phong Lan fan models, 51 Gale models, 15 imported Mitsubishi models, and over 15 fan cage designs, while upgrading technical and quality control (KCS) teams to address quality issues and prevent mass defects.

The company successfully developed new competitive Gale industrial fan models, boosting output in markets such as Hai Phong and Da Nang, while identifying risks from new materials and enforcing thorough testing, trial runs, and stricter workforce management to reduce warranty rates.

The company restructured into five specialized segments—lighting, home appliances, electrical equipment, solar energy, and smart control—while transforming from a pure manufacturer into a technology solutions provider, implementing over 30 automation projects to enhance efficiency and generate significant cost savings.

The company manufactures PCBA and engineering plastic components meeting UL and CE standards, while applying DMS software to enhance customer service efficiency and reduce warranty processing time to 7 days.

The company developed comprehensive smart lighting systems for real estate and smart cities (winning Smart City awards), while adopting modern production management and digital transformation in supporting industries to tightly control costs and product quality.

The company expanded artistic lighting solutions for public projects such as Ben Tre Museum, Ben Thanh Market, and Vinh walking street, while successfully implementing phase one of the Smart Factory initiative across the group.

The company strengthened R&D, partnerships, and the supply of smart, eco-friendly products and solutions to support Net Zero emission goals, while restructuring existing distribution channels to improve marketing and sales efficiency.

Developed new products such as PET preforms, bottles, jars, and caps; successfully produced new types of molds with complex technical requirements.
The company is standardizing production processes and manufacturing technologies (QA, KCS, 5S processes) and implementing an automation program to increase the automation rate from 90% to 95%.

Explored several new products in the molding and bottling segment and continued to produce many new mold sets of various types according to orders.
Continued implementation of the full automation program for all technical processes, continuing the roadmap to increase the automation rate to ≥ 95% across the entire factory.

Developed new products (PET preforms, bottles, jars, and caps) for domestic and export markets, and successfully produced high-tech molds.
Reorganized production equipment according to 5S standards (clean - green - food safety and hygiene) and maintained full automation of the production process.

Explored new molding and bottling products and produced new high-value mold sets.
The company shifted its production technology strategy from semi-automatic/manual processes to a new production process: primarily using automated equipment to produce products, with labor only involved in quality control, inspection, and packaging.

Continued to develop new products (PET preforms, bottles, jars, and caps) and successfully produced complex mold types.
Continuing to implement new production processes (automated production) and assigning QC teams to directly control the input and output processes has helped reduce the defective rate by more than 50%.
The company applied advanced automation technologies to gradually replace manual labor in the production of decorative products.
The company tested and implemented blending processes to utilize low-quality clay as input material.
The company developed multiple new product samples for marketing and exhibition at trade fairs, while investing in machinery, equipment, and supporting tools to reduce manual work in production.

The company developed new eco-friendly and easy-to-assemble product lines with in-house design capabilities, while reorganizing production lines, investing in highly automated machinery, and applying Industry 4.0 technologies in manufacturing management.
The company independently designed and produced product prototypes for market entry, while reinvesting in modern equipment to automate processes and reorganize production.

The company developed self-designed products and showcased new offerings on a 3D showroom, while manufacturing specialized machinery and improving production processes to eliminate redundant steps and reorganize production lines.

The company developed self-designed products for market launch and updated new offerings on a 3D showroom, while investing in modern machinery, producing supporting equipment, and reorganizing production lines.
The company enhanced automation and improved production processes to reduce waste rates and save energy.
The company launched the HCDWOOD outdoor wood-plastic product line, initiating a new factory project and investing in dedicated production lines for these products.

The company planned and began implementing a 9,000-ton/year water disinfectant production line to create a closed production chain and diversify products, while standardizing quality through an advanced membrane-based caustic soda production line to reduce costs.
The company completed a 10-year journey of technological innovation, modernizing and automating all production lines to reduce resource consumption and improve environmental safety.
The company invested in replacing electrolysis equipment and the main transformer system for HCl synthesis, increasing production capacity to 450 tons per day.
The new Vi-CHLORiNE disinfectant product was completed, commissioned, and commercially launched in May 2024, while the company accelerated digital transformation by digitizing records and documents.
The company accelerated the restructuring of production processes and technological improvements.
The company gradually implemented digital transformation across its production, business operations, and management.
The company applied automation across production processes.
Apply technical advancements and improve the production system to meet Vietnamese and international standards such as VietGAP, Global GAP, GACP...

Ladophar aims to be at the forefront of technology, bringing in modern scientific and technical advancements to perfect its production system, meeting Vietnamese and international standards such as VietGAP, Global GAP, GACP, etc.
Establish a Cankina conservation center in Xuan Tho to preserve and store this valuable genetic resource.

LDP launches Lado Collagen, a product in the functional food line, to support body rejuvenation and improve consumer health.
Investing in expanding the area for growing and purchasing medicinal plants, and furthermore focusing on upgrading cultivation areas to GACP-WHO standardization, as well as meeting strict international standards, aims to improve the quantity and quality of input materials, indirectly promoting export sales.

Ladophar has introduced Lado Revive, a dietary supplement specifically designed to support liver detoxification for those who frequently consume alcohol.
Investing in artichoke medicinal plant gardens in Xuan Tho and Lam Ha areas aims to improve the quality of raw materials.

The investment project for a combined steel cutting and coal processing plant in Hai Phong is nearing completion of phase 2. Phase 1 has entered stable operation and achieved profitable growth in 2020.

Investment in research and testing of new products: Talc and PS.
Investment in applying advanced technology to invent new production methods to improve quality.

Started production of new products: moldings and trims.
Operating the supply chain through the application of the 5S management model, lean production, and automation

Preparing to launch the PHALE QUANG HANH mineral water brand.
Pioneering the application of circular production solutions, recycling 100% of consumable materials. Officially operating the production line for new products (high-tech tiling materials) from Q4/2024 with technology combining virgin PVC and nano stone powder. Optimizing the ERP – SAP system to integrate management data.
Officially operating the production line for new high-tech tiling materials
Launch of new innovative products in sizes 80x80, 60x60, and 40x40.
Development of new products including 2cm thick patterned inkjet tiles, 20x120cm wood-grain tiles, 60x120cm tiles, and 90x90cm tiles.
Establishment of a degreasing workshop and relocation of the edge grinding line to optimize processing and packaging.

Successful launch of new ceramic tile products at the Covering exhibition (USA); development of granular tiles, mosaic tiles, and glazed quartz tiles.
Invest $1,500,000 in new equipment for the third factory to improve the production process.

Continued research and development of the following lines: homogeneous tiles, white base tiles, glazed quartz tiles, and granite tiles.
Invest $3,500,000 to improve machinery and production processes. Implement "Total Informatization": Handle the entire process from ordering, planning, production to warehousing on a computer system. Deploy smart energy monitoring technology and switch to using solar power in production.
